meta.name: Chicken Teriyaki
meta.subtitle: Glossy soy-mirin glaze with tender chicken

source.name: ssa-core
notes: Japanese home-style teriyaki; adaptable to thighs or breast; stovetop default.

yield.notes: Serves 4 as a main when paired with rice and a vegetable side.

signals.notes: Sauce keeps well; cooked chicken freezes best without delicate garnish.

tasteProfile.balanceNotes: Classic sweet-salty glaze; adjust sweetness by adding honey/sugar gradually.

notes: Use gluten-free tamari or certified GF soy sauce for gluten-free adaptation.
Confirm mirin brand if strict gluten avoidance is needed.

ingredients.label: Boneless skinless chicken thighs
ingredients.notes: About 1.5 lb. Breast works too; reduce cook time to avoid dryness.

ingredients.label: Kosher salt
ingredients.notes: If soy sauce is very salty, reduce to 1/2 tsp.

ingredients.label: Soy sauce
ingredients.notes: About 1/3 cup. Use tamari for gluten-free.

ingredients.label: Mirin
ingredients.notes: About 1/3 cup. If using 'aji-mirin' (sweetened), reduce added sugar.

ingredients.label: Sake (or dry white wine)
ingredients.notes: About 3 Tbsp. If omitted, add 2 Tbsp water.

ingredients.label: Brown sugar (or honey)
ingredients.notes: Adjust to taste; some mirin is already sweet.

ingredients.label: Cornstarch
ingredients.notes: Optional for extra glossy, faster-thickening sauce.

steps.title: Prep the chicken
instructions: Pat chicken dry with paper towels (dry surface browns better).
Season both sides with salt and pepper.
checks: Chicken is dry to the touch

steps.title: Mix teriyaki sauce
instructions: In a bowl, whisk soy sauce, mirin, sake (or water), brown sugar (or honey), garlic, and ginger until sugar is mostly dissolved.
If using cornstarch, whisk cornstarch with water to make a slurry and set aside.

steps.title: Sear the chicken
instructions: Heat a skillet over medium-high heat. Add neutral oil.
Place chicken smooth-side down. Sear until deeply browned, 3–5 minutes (don’t move it too early).
Flip and cook 2–4 minutes more until mostly cooked through.
checks: Chicken is nearly cooked (about 150–155°F / 65–68°C internal for thighs before glazing)

steps.title: Add sauce and glaze
instructions: Reduce heat to medium. Pour in the sauce.
Simmer, turning chicken to coat, until sauce thickens and turns glossy.
If using cornstarch slurry, add it during simmering and cook 30–60 seconds until shiny.
Continue until chicken reaches 165°F / 74°C and sauce clings to the chicken.
checks: Sauce coats the back of a spoon

steps.title: Slice and serve
instructions: Spoon extra glaze over the top.
Garnish with scallions and toasted sesame seeds.
Serve with rice and a simple vegetable (e.g., steamed broccoli, sautéed bok choy, or cucumber salad).

pairings.notes: Balance sweet-salty teriyaki with something light and acidic (pickles/salad) and a simple vegetable.

refrigerator.notes: Store chicken with sauce in an airtight container. Reheat gently to avoid sugar scorching.

freezer.notes: Freeze sliced chicken with sauce. Thaw overnight in fridge; reheat gently with a splash of water.

methods: Skillet over low heat with 1–2 Tbsp water, covered
Microwave in short bursts, stirring sauce between bursts

planner.batchNotes: Double the sauce and cook extra chicken for rice bowls and salads.

notes: If using chicken breast: pound to even thickness; reduce cook time; pull at 160°F and rest to 165°F carryover.
For less sweet teriyaki: start with 1 Tbsp sugar and adjust after reduction.
For classic simplicity: omit garlic/ginger; rely on soy+mirin+sake+sugar.
Start skin-side down; render on medium heat 6–8 minutes until crisp.
Pour off excess fat before adding sauce to prevent greasy glaze.
Sear salmon 2–3 minutes per side depending on thickness.
Use tamari or certified gluten-free soy sauce.

summary: Classic stovetop chicken teriyaki: seared chicken finished in a glossy soy-mirin glaze. Weeknight-friendly and great for rice bowls.

handling: Keep raw chicken separate from ready-to-eat foods.
Avoid cross-contamination: wash hands, boards, and knives after handling raw poultry.
